rulings.Case Title: CARMELA ABALON VS. MICHELLE TORRES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of And Mtn To Compel 1) Written Discovery Verifications 2) To Have Req.'S For Admissions Deemed Admitted; Req. For Reasonable Sanctions And Attorney'S
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, June 04, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANTs MICHELLE TORRES, VICTORIA TORRES' Motion To Compel 1) Written Discovery Verifications 2) To Have Requests For Admissions Deemed Admitted; Request For Reasonable Sanctions And Attorney'S Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DEFENDANTS MICHAEL TORRES AND VISSTORIA TORRES' MOTION TO COMPEL THE FILING OF A WRITTEN VERIFICATION TO PLAINTIFF CARMELA ABALON'S DISCOVERY REQUESTS AND TO HAVE HER REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION DEEMED ADMITTED IS GRANTED IN PART. PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO FILE THE WRITTEN VERIFICATION WITHIN TEN (10) DAYS OF THIS ORDER; FAILURE TO PROVIDE WRITTEN VERIFICATION TO THE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION WITHIN THAT TEN DAY PERIOD WILL RESULT IN THE MATTERS BEING DEEMED ADMITTED AND PLAINTIFF WILL BE PRECLUDED FROM INTRODUCING EVIDENCE SEEKING TO CONTRADICT SUCH ADMISSIONS. MONETARY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $500.00 WILL BE ASSESSED AGAINST PLAINTIFF PAYMENT OF WHICH SHOULD BE MADE WITHIN TNE (10) DAYS OF THIS ORDER. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANTS SHOULD PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER, SUBMIT TO COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND PRESENT TO THE COURT FOR SIGNATURE AT THE HEARING ON JUNE 4, 2014. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, June 04, 2014, Line 2. DEFENDANTS DAVID DEMO and SUSAN DEMO's Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses, Production Of Documents And Request For Fees, Costs And Monetary Sanctions Against Henrietta Martin And Her Attorney Of Record.(Part 1 of 2) Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the D ept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DEFENDANT DAVID DEMO'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO HIS FORM INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE; SPECCIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE; REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE AND REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS, SET ONE IF GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART AS FOLLOWS; 1. MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS NOS. 1-44 IS DENIED. IT APPEARS FROM THE PAPERS THAT PLAINTIFF HAS PRODUCED ALL RESPONSIVE DOCUMENT AND IS WITHHOLDING NONE BASED UPON THE OBJECTIONS STATED IN HER RESPONSES. 2. PLAINTIF'S OBJECTIONS TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS NOS 45, 46 & 46 ARE OVERRULED AND FULL AND COMPLETE RESPONSES WILL BE PROVIDED TO DEFENDANT WITHIN TWENTY (20) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. 3. PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO SERVE WITHIN TWENTY (20) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER A VERIFICATION OF ALL HER SUPPLEMENTAL DISCOVERY RESPONSES AND BASED ON AN ASSUMPTION THAT PLAINTIFF WILL COMPLY DEFENDANT'S MOTION IS DENIED AS TO NOS. 6, 8, 28, 31, 43, 55, 64,66, 67, 68, & 69. PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO SERVE WITHIN TWENTY (20) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER A RESPONSE TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NO. 7 PLAIINTIFF HAS SUPPLEMENTED HER RESPONSES TO DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS NOS 20, 21, 22, 23, 24, 31, 32, 33 & 34. TO THE EXTENT THAT ANY OF THE RESPONSES ARE OTHER THAN UNQUALIFIED ADMISSIONS, PLAINTIIFF WITHIN TWENTY (20) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER WILPROVIDE TO DEFENDANT THE INFORMATION CALLED FOR BY FORM INTERROGATORY 17.1. 4. PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO SERVE WITHIN TWENTY (20) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER FULL AND COMPLETE RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES NOS. 2.2, 2.3, 2.4, 2.6, 9.1, 17.1 AND 50.2 5. MONETARY SANCTIONS ARE ASSESSED AGAINST PLAINTIFF IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,500.00 WITH PAYMENT TO BE MADE TO DEFENDANT AND HIS COUNSEL WITHIN TEN (10) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. 6. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT WILL PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER, SUBMIT TO COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNATURE AT THE HEARING ON JUNE 4, 2014. (end of Part 1, see part 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: YEFIM SHEYNIS VS. MAZZETTI NASH LIPSEY BURCH INC., et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Mazzetti Nash Lipsey Burch Inc.'S Motion For Summary Judgment / Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 4, 2014, Line 10: Defendant Mazzetti Nash Lipsey Burch Inc.'S Motion For Summary Judgment / Adjudication. The motion is granted in part and denied in part. Summary judgment is denied because disputed issues of material fact exist as to at least one cause of action. Summary adjudication is granted in favor of Defendant as to Plaintiff's claims for harassment and retaliation. Demotions are specifically listed as actions that do not come within the meaning of harassment, and are not actionable under a harassment cause of action. (Janken v. GM Hughes Electronics (1996) 46 Cal.App.4th 55, 63.) Moreover, the allegedly harassing statements fall short of the required showing of a "pattern of continuous, pervasive, harassment." (Mokler v. County of Orange (2007) 157 Cal.App.4th 121, 141-42.) As to the retaliation claim, the adverse action occurred nearly a year after Plaintiff allegedly made the complaint, and there is no other evidence to support the contention that Plaintiff's email to Karmiris motivated his termination. However, summary adjudication is denied as to Plaintiff's claims for age discrimination, failure to prevent discrimination, and wrongful termination. Plaintiff has established that he suffered adverse action when he was demoted and terminated, and that there is a triable issue of material fact as to the presence of a discriminatory motive and whether Defendant's proffered explanation was pretext for discrimination. (Def.'s Resp. to Pl.'s Separate Statement in Supp. Of Mot. For Summ. J. # 10, 18, 20, and 24.) Moreover, there are triable issues of material fact as to whether Plaintiff's email to Karmiris constituted a sufficient complaint of discrimination, and whether Vernon had a duty to either investigate or inform another employee of the complaint, therefore foreclosing summary adjudication on the failure to prevent discrimination claim. (Id. # 36, 41.) Finally, Defendant relies solely on the failure of Plaintiff's other claims to support summary adjudication of the wrongful termination claim. Thus, because Plaintiff has a viable claim for age discrimination, summary adjudication on this cause of action is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JUAN CARLOS ARANA MORENO VS. IGNITE RESTAURANT GROUP, INC DBA JOE'S CRAB SHACK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Foar Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 4, 2014, Line 3: PLAINTIFFS JUAN CARLOS ARANA MORENO, JULIAN POOT, LIBORIO CHAN, LUIS ALBERTO COLLI PERERA, EDGAR DIAZ, JOSE MENCIA, RAUL ENRIQUE MISEK, SAMIR ROQUE, and JUAN ANTONIO SALDIVCAR'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint. The motion is granted. The court has jurisdiction over this case after the district court ordered the case remanded to state court. There is a liberal policy in favor of amendment, the amendments do not change the tenor of the case, and the amendments will not prejudice defendant. The court denies defendant's request to indefinitely defer ruling on this motion. Defendant could have sought to stay the district court's order of remand in federal court. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: PORTFOLIO RECOVERY ASSOCIATES, LLC VS. DANIEL L. SCHUTTE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Further Resposnes To Special Interoggs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, June 04, 2014, Line 8, DEFENDANT DANIEL SCHUTTE's Motion To Compel Further Resposnes To Special Interogatories Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DEFENDANT DANIEL SCHUTTE'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NOS 1 - 6, AND 10 -12 IS GRANTED AS FOLLOWS: 1. IN RESPONSE TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NOS 1, 4, 5 &6 PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED AND PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO PROVIDE TO DEFENDANT THE IDENTITY, INCLUDING NAMES AND CONTACT INFORMATION , OF ALL PERSONS KNOWN TO DEFENDANT AS CALLED FOR BY EACH SUCH INTERROGATORY. 2. IN RESPONSE TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NOS 2 & 3 PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED AND PLAINTIFF IS ORDER TO PROVIDE DEFENDANT WITH IDENTITY OF THE DOCUMENTS, INCLUDING DESCRIPTION OR TITLE OF THE DOCUMENT AND THE NAME AND CONTACT INFORMATION OF THE CUSTODIAN OF THE DOCUMENT. 3. IN RESPONSE TO INTERROGATORIES NOS 10 & 11 PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED AND PLAINTIFF WILL SET WITH SPECIFICITY THE DATE WHICH IT CONTENDS THE ACCOUNT WAS SENT TO DEFFENDANT AND THE DATE OF THE LAST ITEM ON THE ACCOUNT. 4. IN RESPONSE TO INTERROGATORY NO. 12 PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED AND PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO PROVIDE DEFENDANT WITH THE IDENTITY OF THE DOCUMENTS, INCLUDING DESCRIPTION AND THE NAME AND CONTACT INFORMATION OF THE CUSTODIAN OF THE DOCUMENT. 5. ALL RESPONSES ORDERED IN PARAGRAPHS 1- 4 ABOVE WILL BE DELIVERED TO DEFENDENT WITHIN THIRTY (30) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. 6. NO MONETARY SANCTIONS WILL BE ASSESSED. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT WILL PREPARE AND APPROPRIATE ORDER, SUBMIT IT TO COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER IT TO THE COURT AT THE HEARING ON JUNE 4, 2014. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, June 04, 2014, Line 9, PLAINTIFF PORTFOLIO RECOVERY ASSOCIATES ,LLC's Motion For Order That Matters In Request For Admission Of Truth Of Facts Be Admitted And For Monetary Sanction Against Defendant Cherry L Staana Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: PLAINTIFF PORTFOLLIO RECOVERY ASSOCIATES LLC'S MOTION IS DENIED AS MOOT; IT APPEARS FROM THE PAPERS THAT DEFENDANT HAS COMPLIED WITH THE RELIEF REQUESTED. rulings.Case Title: CLARENCE WILSON et al VS. 1049 MARKET STREET, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Hearing Of Defendant 1049 Market Street, Llc'S Motion To Strike Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 4, 2014, Line 8: Hearing Of Defendant 1049 Market Street, LLC'S Motion To Strike Complaint. Denied. The Court must engage in a two-step process when ruling on an Anti-SLAPP motion under CCP sec. 425.16. The first prong is met if the moving party meets the burden of making a prima facie showing that the plaintiff's cause of action arises out of an act in furtherance of a person's right to petition or free speech under the US or CA Const. in connection with a public issue. (CCP sec. 425.16(b)(1).) Defendant has not made a prima facie showing that any of the causes of action arise from protected activity. Although Defendant argues that Plaintiffs filed this suit in order to chill its Constitutionally protected right to petition, i.e. filing eviction notices, "[o]ur Supreme Court has made it clear that a plaintiff's cause of action does not necessarily arise from a defendant's section 425.16 protected activity merely because the plaintiff's suit was filed after the defendant engaged in that activity." (Oviedo v. Windsor Twelve Properties, LLC (2012) 212 Cal.App.4th 97, 110.) Rather, "the defendant's act underlying the plaintiff's cause of action must itself have been an act in furtherance of the right of petition or free speech." (Id.) Here, all of the causes of action arise from Defendant's alleged failure to maintain the habitability of the building in an effort to pressure the Plaintiffs to voluntarily move out. Because the first prong is not met here, the Court need not analyze the second prong. Plaintiffs' request for attorneys' fees is denied. The motion is not frivolous and there is no evidence that the motion was filed to solely cause delay. (CCP sec. 425.16(c)(1).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
